arguments.file_text:
# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)

## Current State
- 5 MSTest test projects on .NET 8 SDK
- NuGet packages: `MSTest.TestFramework 3.8.0`, `MSTest.TestAdapter 3.8.0`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0`
- CI: Azure DevOps with `VSTest@3` task, TRX reporting, code coverage

## Approach
Upgrade to **MSTest 4.x** (the MSTest SDK), which ships Microsoft.Testing.Platform as the default runner. This replaces the VSTest host entirely. Then update CI to use `dotnet test` instead of the `VSTest@3` task.

---

## Phase 1 — Centralise package versions in `Directory.Build.props`

If not already done, create or update a `Directory.Build.props` at the solution root so every change below is made once.

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

---

## Phase 2 — Upgrade each test `.csproj`

### 2a. Remove old packages
Remove these three `<PackageReference>` entries from every test project:

```
MSTest.TestFramework  3.8.0
MSTest.TestAdapter    3.8.0
Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0
```

### 2b. Switch to the MSTest SDK (option A — recommended)
Change each test project's `<Project Sdk="...">` line:

```xml
<!-- Before -->
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

<!-- After -->
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/3.8.3">   <!-- latest stable 4.x-era SDK; check NuGet -->
```

This single SDK reference replaces all three removed packages and automatically enables
Microsoft.Testing.Platform as the runner.

> **Alternative (option B):** Keep `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` and add:
> ```xml
> <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="4.3.0" />   <!-- single meta-package -->
> <PropertyGroup>
>   <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
>   <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
> </PropertyGroup>
> ```
> Option B is viable but Option A is simpler.

### 2c. Add TRX + code coverage extensions
With the MSTest SDK you add extensions via properties or packages:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <!-- Produces .trx files (replaces VSTest TRX logger) -->
  <EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>true</EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>
  <EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsTrxReport>true</EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsTrxReport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

If using Option B instead, add the equivalent NuGet packages:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.3" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
```

---

## Phase 3 — Verify locally

### 3a. Build
```bash
dotnet build MySolution.sln
```
Each test project now compiles as an **executable** (the MTP runner is embedded).

### 3b. Run tests via `dotnet test`
```bash
dotnet test MySolution.sln --logger trx --results-directory ./TestResults
```
Confirm:
- All tests discover and pass.
- `.trx` files appear in `./TestResults`.
- No warnings about "VSTest mode" or "bridge mode".

### 3c. Run tests directly (optional validation)
Each test project is now a standalone exe:
```bash
./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyTests --list-tests
./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyTests --report-trx
```

---

## Phase 4 — Update Azure DevOps pipeline

### 4a. Replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`
```yaml
# BEFORE
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testSelector: 'testAssemblies'
    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
    searchFolder: '$(System.DefaultWorkingDirectory)'
    resultsFolder: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true

# AFTER
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --configuration Release
      --logger trx
      --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults
```

### 4b. Publish TRX results (unchanged)
```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Test Results'
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'   # TRX format is still "VSTest" in this task
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.trx'
  condition: always()
```

### 4c. Publish code coverage
```yaml
- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Code Coverage'
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
  condition: always()
```

---

## Phase 5 — Clean up

- Remove any leftover `.runsettings` file entries that reference VSTest-specific adapters.
- If you had a `<TestAdapterPath>` in your `.runsettings`, remove it.
- Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` from any `Directory.Packages.props` / central package management.
- Search solution for stale references: `MSTest.TestAdapter`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`.

---

## Phase 6 — Optional enhancements (post-migration)

| Enhancement | How |
|---|---|
| Parallel test execution (per-project) | `--maximum-failed-tests 0` flag or `[Parallelize]` assembly attribute still works |
| Hot-reload tests | `dotnet test --hot-reload` (SDK 9+) |
| Native AOT test support | Set `<PublishAot>true</PublishAot>` (MTP supports it, VSTest does not) |
| Custom test reports | Add `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.Retry` or other MTP extensions |
| Playwright / integration tests | MTP supports long-running tests better than VSTest |

---

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Custom VSTest adapters / data collectors break | Check if MTP equivalents exist; keep bridge mode (`<TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>`) as fallback |
| `.runsettings` incompatibility | MTP ignores some VSTest-only runsettings nodes; review and simplify |
| Third-party test loggers (e.g., NUnit logger) | Replace with MTP extension equivalents |
| `dotnet test` bridge mode auto-enabled | Ensure `EnableMSTestRunner` is true so MTP runs natively, not via the VSTest bridge |

---

## TODO Checklist

1. Create / update `Directory.Build.props` with shared test properties
2. Migrate `.csproj` files (remove old packages, switch SDK) — repeat × 5
3. Add TRX + code coverage extensions
4. Validate locally: build, run tests, check `.trx` output
5. Update Azure DevOps YAML: replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`
6. Update test-results and code-coverage publish tasks
7. Clean up stale references (`.runsettings`, package management)
8. Run full CI pipeline and verify results parity

- NuGet packages: `MSTest.TestFramework 3.8.0`, `MSTest.TestAdapter 3.8.0`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0`
- CI: Azure DevOps with `VSTest@3` task, TRX reporting, code coverage

## Approach
Upgrade to **MSTest 4.x** (the MSTest SDK), which ships Microsoft.Testing.Platform as the default runner. This replaces the VSTest host entirely. Then update CI to use `dotnet test` instead of the `VSTest@3` task.

---

## Phase 1 — Centralise package versions in `Directory.Build.props`

If not already done, create or update a `Directory.Build.props` at the solution root so every change below is made once.

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

---

## Phase 2 — Upgrade each test `.csproj`

### 2a. Remove old packages
Remove these three `<PackageReference>` entries from every test project:

```
MSTest.TestFramework  3.8.0
MSTest.TestAdapter    3.8.0
Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0
```

### 2b. Switch to the MSTest SDK (option A — recommended)
Change each test project's `<Project Sdk="...">` line:

```xml
<!-- Before -->
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

<!-- After -->
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/3.8.3">   <!-- latest stable 4.x-era SDK; check NuGet -->
```

This single SDK reference replaces all three removed packages and automatically enables
Microsoft.Testing.Platform as the runner.

> **Alternative (option B):** Keep `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` and add:
> ```xml
> <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="4.3.0" />   <!-- single meta-package -->
> <PropertyGroup>
>   <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
>   <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
> </PropertyGroup>
> ```
> Option B is viable but Option A is simpler.

With the MSTest SDK you add extensions via properties or packages:

<PropertyGroup>

  <EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>true</EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>
  <EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsTrxReport>true</EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsTrxReport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.3" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
```

dotnet build MySolution.sln

### 4c. Publish code coverage

- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2

# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)

## Current State
- 5 MSTest test projects on .NET 8 SDK
- NuGet packages: `MSTest.TestFramework 3.8.0`, `MSTest.TestAdapter 3.8.0`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0`
- CI: Azure DevOps with `VSTest@3` task, TRX reporting, code coverage

## Approach
Upgrade to **MSTest SDK 3.8.x** (or the `MSTest` 4.x meta-package), which ships Microsoft.Testing.Platform as the default runner. This replaces the VSTest host entirely. Then update CI to use `dotnet test` instead of the `VSTest@3` task.

---

## Phase 1 — Centralise package versions in `Directory.Build.props`

If not already done, create or update a `Directory.Build.props` at the solution root so every change below is made once.

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

---

## Phase 2 — Upgrade each test `.csproj`

### 2a. Remove old packages
Remove these three `<PackageReference>` entries from every test project:

```
MSTest.TestFramework  3.8.0
MSTest.TestAdapter    3.8.0
Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0
```

### 2b. Switch to the MSTest SDK (Option A — recommended)
Change each test project's `<Project Sdk="...">` line:

```xml
<!-- Before -->
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

<!-- After -->
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/3.8.3">
```

This single SDK reference replaces all three removed packages and automatically enables
Microsoft.Testing.Platform as the runner. Check NuGet for the latest `MSTest.Sdk` version.

> **Alternative (Option B):** Keep `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` and add:
> ```xml
> <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="4.3.0" />
> <PropertyGroup>
>   <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
>   <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
> </PropertyGroup>
> ```
> Option B is viable but Option A is simpler.

### 2c. Add TRX + code coverage extensions
With the MSTest SDK, enable extensions via properties in `Directory.Build.props` (or per-project):

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>true</EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>
  <EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsTrxReport>true</EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsTrxReport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

If using Option B instead, add the equivalent NuGet packages:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.3" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
```

---

## Phase 3 — Verify locally

### 3a. Build
```bash
dotnet build MySolution.sln
```
Each test project now compiles as an **executable** (the MTP runner is embedded).

### 3b. Run tests via `dotnet test`
```bash
dotnet test MySolution.sln --logger trx --results-directory ./TestResults
```
Confirm:
- All tests discover and pass.
- `.trx` files appear in `./TestResults`.
- No warnings about "VSTest mode" or "bridge mode".

### 3c. Run tests directly (optional sanity check)
Each test project is now a standalone exe:
```bash
./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyTests --list-tests
./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyTests --report-trx
```

---

## Phase 4 — Update Azure DevOps pipeline

### 4a. Replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`
```yaml
# BEFORE
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testSelector: 'testAssemblies'
    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
    searchFolder: '$(System.DefaultWorkingDirectory)'
    resultsFolder: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true

# AFTER
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --configuration Release
      --logger trx
      --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults
```

### 4b. Publish TRX results (unchanged)
```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Test Results'
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'   # TRX format is still "VSTest" here
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.trx'
  condition: always()
```

### 4c. Publish code coverage
```yaml
- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Code Coverage'
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
  condition: always()
```

---

## Phase 5 — Clean up

- Remove any `.runsettings` entries that reference VSTest-specific adapters or `<TestAdapterPath>`.
- Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` / `MSTest.TestAdapter` from `Directory.Packages.props` if using central package management.
- Search the solution for stale references: `MSTest.TestAdapter`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`.

---

## Phase 6 — Optional enhancements (post-migration)

| Enhancement | How |
|---|---|
| Native AOT test support | `<PublishAot>true</PublishAot>` — MTP supports it, VSTest does not |
| Retry failed tests | `EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsRetry` property |
| Crash dump on failure | `EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCrashDump` property |
| Hang dump on timeout | `EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsHangDump` property |

---

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Custom VSTest data collectors break | Check for MTP equivalents; temporarily use bridge mode (`<TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>`) |
| `.runsettings` incompatibility | MTP ignores some VSTest-only nodes; review and simplify |
| Third-party test loggers | Replace with MTP extension equivalents |
| `dotnet test` falls back to VSTest bridge | Ensure `EnableMSTestRunner` is `true` so MTP runs natively |

---

## TODO Checklist

1. Create / update `Directory.Build.props` with shared test properties
2. Migrate `.csproj` files — remove old packages, switch to `MSTest.Sdk` (× 5 projects)
3. Add TRX + code coverage extension properties
4. Validate locally: build, run tests, verify `.trx` output
5. Update Azure DevOps YAML: replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`
6. Update test-results and code-coverage publish tasks
7. Clean up stale references (`.runsettings`, package management)
8. Run full CI pipeline and verify results parity

# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)

## Current State
- 5 MSTest test projects on .NET 8 SDK
- NuGet packages: `MSTest.TestFramework 3.8.0`, `MSTest.TestAdapter 3.8.0`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0`
- CI: Azure DevOps with `VSTest@3` task, TRX reporting, code coverage

## Approach
Upgrade to **MSTest SDK 3.8.x** (or the `MSTest` 4.x meta-package), which ships Microsoft.Testing.Platform as the default runner. This replaces the VSTest host entirely. Then update CI to use `dotnet test` instead of the `VSTest@3` task.

---

## Phase 1 — Centralise package versions in `Directory.Build.props`

If not already done, create or update a `Directory.Build.props` at the solution root so every change below is made once.

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

---

## Phase 2 — Upgrade each test `.csproj`

### 2a. Remove old packages
Remove these three `<PackageReference>` entries from every test project:

```
MSTest.TestFramework  3.8.0
MSTest.TestAdapter    3.8.0
Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0
```

### 2b. Switch to the MSTest SDK (Option A — recommended)
Change each test project's `<Project Sdk="...">` line:

```xml
<!-- Before -->
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

<!-- After -->
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/3.8.3">
```

This single SDK reference replaces all three removed packages and automatically enables
Microsoft.Testing.Platform as the runner. Check NuGet for the latest `MSTest.Sdk` version.

> **Alternative (Option B):** Keep `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` and add:
> ```xml
> <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="4.3.0" />
> <PropertyGroup>
>   <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
>   <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
> </PropertyGroup>
> ```
> Option B is viable but Option A is simpler.

### 2c. Add TRX + code coverage extensions
With the MSTest SDK, enable extensions via properties in `Directory.Build.props` (or per-project):

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>true</EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCodeCoverage>
  <EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsTrxReport>true</EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsTrxReport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

If using Option B instead, add the equivalent NuGet packages:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.3" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
```

---

## Phase 3 — Verify locally

### 3a. Build
```bash
dotnet build MySolution.sln
```
Each test project now compiles as an **executable** (the MTP runner is embedded).

### 3b. Run tests via `dotnet test`
```bash
dotnet test MySolution.sln --logger trx --results-directory ./TestResults
```
Confirm:
- All tests discover and pass.
- `.trx` files appear in `./TestResults`.
- No warnings about "VSTest mode" or "bridge mode".

### 3c. Run tests directly (optional sanity check)
Each test project is now a standalone exe:
```bash
./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyTests --list-tests
./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyTests --report-trx
```

---

## Phase 4 — Update Azure DevOps pipeline

### 4a. Replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`
```yaml
# BEFORE
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testSelector: 'testAssemblies'
    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
    searchFolder: '$(System.DefaultWorkingDirectory)'
    resultsFolder: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true

# AFTER
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --configuration Release
      --logger trx
      --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults
```

### 4b. Publish TRX results (unchanged)
```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Test Results'
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'   # TRX format is still "VSTest" here
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.trx'
  condition: always()
```

### 4c. Publish code coverage
```yaml
- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Code Coverage'
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
  condition: always()
```

---

## Phase 5 — Clean up

- Remove any `.runsettings` entries that reference VSTest-specific adapters or `<TestAdapterPath>`.
- Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` / `MSTest.TestAdapter` from `Directory.Packages.props` if using central package management.
- Search the solution for stale references: `MSTest.TestAdapter`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`.

---

## Phase 6 — Optional enhancements (post-migration)

| Enhancement | How |
|---|---|
| Native AOT test support | `<PublishAot>true</PublishAot>` — MTP supports it, VSTest does not |
| Retry failed tests | `EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsRetry` property |
| Crash dump on failure | `EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsCrashDump` property |
| Hang dump on timeout | `EnableMicrosoftTestingExtensionsHangDump` property |

---

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Custom VSTest data collectors break | Check for MTP equivalents; temporarily use bridge mode (`<TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>`) |
| `.runsettings` incompatibility | MTP ignores some VSTest-only nodes; review and simplify |
| Third-party test loggers | Replace with MTP extension equivalents |
| `dotnet test` falls back to VSTest bridge | Ensure `EnableMSTestRunner` is `true` so MTP runs natively |

---

## TODO Checklist

1. Create / update `Directory.Build.props` with shared test properties
2. Migrate `.csproj` files — remove old packages, switch to `MSTest.Sdk` (× 5 projects)
3. Add TRX + code coverage extension properties
4. Validate locally: build, run tests, verify `.trx` output
5. Update Azure DevOps YAML: replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`
6. Update test-results and code-coverage publish tasks
7. Clean up stale references (`.runsettings`, package management)
8. Run full CI pipeline and verify results parity
